label: Recent event
query: Who won the most recent Super Bowl?
response_text:
The Seattle Seahawks won the most recent Super Bowl, defeating the New England Patriots 29-13 in Super Bowl LX on February 8, 2026, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California. ([time.com](https://time.com/7372995/super-bowl-winner-2026-seattle/?utm_source=openai)) This victory marked the Seahawks' second Super Bowl title, with their first win occurring in 2014. Running back Kenneth Walker III was named the game's Most Valuable Player (MVP) after rushing for 135 yards, becoming the first running back to earn the MVP title since Terrell Davis 28 years prior. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Super_Bowl_LX?utm_source=openai))

The Seahawks' defense was dominant, holding the Patriots scoreless through the first three quarters. A pivotal moment came when linebacker Uchenna Nwosu returned a fumble for a touchdown late in the fourth quarter. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/local/seattle/2026/02/09/super-bowl-lx-results-seahawks-patriots?utm_source=openai)) Additionally, kicker Jason Myers set a Super Bowl record by drilling five field goals during the game.

response_text: The capital of France is Paris.

label: Open-ended research query
query: Summarize the latest developments in EU AI regulation.
search_queries_issued: latest developments in EU AI regulation 2023
response_text:
The European Union (EU) has been actively advancing its regulatory framework for artificial intelligence (AI) to ensure the development and deployment of trustworthy AI systems that align with democratic values and fundamental rights. Key developments include:

**Artificial Intelligence Act (AI Act):**

- **Enactment and Phased Implementation:** The AI Act entered into force on August 1, 2024, with a structured timeline for application:
  - **February 2, 2025:** Prohibitions on certain AI practices and AI literacy obligations became applicable.
  - **August 2, 2025:** Governance rules and obligations for general-purpose AI (GPAI) models took effect.
  - **August 2, 2026:** Full applicability of the AI Act, with specific provisions for high-risk AI systems integrated into regulated products extended until August 2, 2027. ([digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai?utm_source=openai))

- **Risk-Based Classification:** The Act categorizes AI systems into

label: search_context_size: low
query: What are the latest AI safety news headlines?
response_text:
Here are some recent developments in AI safety:

**Legislative Actions:**

- **California's Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act:** Enacted in September 2025, this law mandates that AI companies publicly assess potential catastrophic risks of their models and document adherence to safety standards. It also establishes whistleblower protections and procedures for reporting critical safety incidents. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transparency_in_Frontier_Artificial_Intelligence_Act?utm_source=openai))

- **New York's Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act:** Signed into law in December 2025, the RAISE Act imposes transparency, safety, and reporting requirements on developers of large AI models. It aligns closely with California's legislation, emphasizing the need for public risk assessments and adherence to safety protocols. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Responsible_AI_Safety_and_Education_Act?utm_source=openai))

**Industry Developments:**

- **Oracle's AI-Enabled Safety Management in Construction:** In March 2026, Oracle introduced an AI-driven predictive intelligence solution aimed at enhancing safety management in the construction industry. This tool helps organizations forecast and prevent safety incidents, promoting a proactive safety culture.

label: search_context_size: high
query: What are the latest AI safety news headlines?
response_text:
Here are some recent developments in AI safety:

**Regulatory Actions:**

- **California's Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act:** In September 2025, California enacted SB-53, requiring AI companies to publicly assess and document potential catastrophic risks associated with their models. This law also establishes whistleblower protections and mandates reporting of critical safety incidents. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transparency_in_Frontier_Artificial_Intelligence_Act?utm_source=openai))

- **New York's Responsible AI Safety and Education Act (RAISE Act):** Signed into law in December 2025, the RAISE Act imposes transparency and safety requirements on developers of large AI models. It mandates public safety frameworks, incident reporting within 72 hours, and establishes a dedicated enforcement office within the Department of Financial Services. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Responsible_AI_Safety_and_Education_Act?utm_source=openai))

**Industry Developments:**

- **Oracle's AI-Enabled Safety Management:** In March 2026, Oracle introduced the Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety, an AI-driven solution designed to predict and prevent safety incidents in construction projects, aiming to foster a proactive safety culture.

label: Ambiguous query
response_text:
As of March 11, 2026, the latest stable release of Python is version 3.13.6, which was released on August 6, 2025. ([discuss.python.org](https://discuss.python.org/t/python-3-13-6-has-been-released/101482?utm_source=openai)) This release includes numerous bug fixes, build improvements, and documentation updates.

Python 3.13 introduced several new features and optimizations compared to its predecessor, Python 3.12. Notably, it includes enhancements to error messaging, more flexible parsing of f-strings, support for the buffer protocol in Python code, a new debugging and profiling API, and the ability to use isolated subinterpreters with separate Global Interpreter Locks. ([pythonnews.org](https://www.pythonnews.org/python-3-12-0-stable-release-launched/?utm_source=openai))

The next planned release is Python 3.13.7, scheduled for August 14, 2025. ([peps.python.org](https://peps.python.org/pep-0719/?utm_source=openai)) For the most current information and to download the latest version, you can visit the official Python website.
